intro: 756 AI items this week (62 India-relevant) across 10 sources.

items.summary: arXiv:2606.29247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models represent a promising direction for embodied intelligence in surgical robotics. Despite the prevalence of VLA benchmarks for general robotics, standardized evaluation platforms specifically des…

items.title: QED: An Open-Source Multi-Agent System for Generating Mathematical Proofs on Open Problems
items.summary: arXiv:2604.24021v4 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We present QED, an open-source multi-agent system that turns human-provided research questions into complete mathematical proofs without further human guidance. Its pipeline is designed to overcome common failures of single-q…

items.title: Unlocking Britain’s next era of productivity: Building a nation of AI trailblazers
items.summary: Google UK shares its latest Economic Impact Report and how to enable more people to unlock the benefits of AI-powered technologies.

items.title: The AI jobs debate just got messier
items.summary: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.

items.summary: arXiv:2606.28747v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent artificial intelligence (AI) systems have shown remarkable progress in mathematical reasoning. Many existing approaches, including large language models (LLMs), draw on human prior knowledge in the form of mathematical tex…

items.title: MedEvoEval: Evaluating Continual Evolution of Doctor Agents through Simulated Clinical Episodes
items.summary: arXiv:2606.28900v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Doctor agents are moving beyond single-turn answer generation toward evolving clinical decision systems. Within an outpatient episode, they acquire evidence, use examination and consultation resources, and decide when to finalize…

items.title: Vocus plans $343 million Sydney-Melbourne fibre link to meet AI demand
items.summary: Macquarie-backed Vocus is set to invest A$500 million in a new fibre network connecting Sydney and Melbourne by 2029. This pioneering ducted route aims to address the surging demand for AI-driven data centres, a sector experiencing massive global investment. The project is exp…

items.title: How global scammers use US tech to fleece people
items.summary: Indian scammer Safeer Koorimannil reveals how AI from American tech giants fuels global fraud rings in Myanmar. Trafficked to a scam center, he used sophisticated software to target thousands daily. The investigation highlights how U.S. technology, from AI models to internet i…

items.title: Bitcoin miner and AI firm Ionic Digital files for Nasdaq direct listing
items.summary: The company was formed in January 2024 to acquire the cryptocurrency mining assets of Celsius Mining, a subsidiary of ‌Celsius, which ⁠received ⁠U.S. bankruptcy court approval for a restructuring in November 2023.

items.title: Cyber talent gap widens as demand for hybrid skills outpaces supply
items.summary: "Modern cybersecurity sits at the intersection of digital platforms, AI deployment, regulatory accountability, operational resilience and customer trust.

items.title: Digital Realty to pay Blackstone $3.5 billion for stake in Virginia data centers
items.summary: The move strengthens the data center operator's position in Northern ​Virginia, the world's largest data center market, where demand for capacity has surged ​as cloud computing and AI drive higher infrastructure needs.

items.title: Apple says it is releasing updates early in response to AI cybersecurity concerns
items.summary: The shift marks a notable change ⁠in Apple's ‌longstanding practice of packaging security fixes with ​broader software ​releases, an acknowledgment that AI is compressing ⁠the window attackers need to exploit known flaws.

careers.note: Demand snapshot for 2026. ML Engineer is the most in-demand AI title; agentic-AI and deep-learning skills carry the highest pay premiums; Python + one cloud is the baseline. Specialisation beats generalisation for freshers.

paths.note: #1 AI title — build, deploy & maintain models in production. Best all-round entry into AI.
RAG, tool-use & multi-agent systems. ~40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by year-end.
Neural nets for vision & speech. Highest premiums; usually needs strong maths / grad study.
Ship & scale models on the cloud. Cloud AI certs add 20–25% to salary.
Foundational, widest entry door; a reliable first step into AI.
Imaging, robotics, autonomous systems, medical AI.
Powers chatbots, search & agents — the hottest application area.
5–8 yrs experience; $140k–$332k bands. Translates AI into business value.
Alignment, evals, red-teaming & policy — newer but expanding fast.

fresher.why: The lingua franca of ML — non-negotiable.
Linear algebra, probability & calculus — enough to reason about models.
Regression, classification, evaluation, the ML workflow.
Build & train neural nets; PyTorch dominates research & jobs.
Ship a small LLM app — the most visible skill to recruiters now.
Real AI work is mostly data; SQL is everywhere.
AWS leads (~40% of postings), then Azure & GCP. Version everything.
A deployed project that solves a real problem is the #1 differentiator.

experienced.why: Docker, CI/CD, monitoring, model/versioning — production is where value lives.
Multi-agent orchestration, tool-use, evals — the surging frontier.
AWS/Azure/GCP architecture; certs add a 20–25% salary premium.
Adapt, measure and harden models for a domain.
Cost & latency engineering for large models.
Trade-offs across data, latency, cost & reliability at scale.
CV, NLP, recsys, healthcare — depth beats breadth at senior levels.
Leading teams and tying models to measurable outcomes.

profile.name: Harman Singh Sandhu
profile.blurb: I build AI/ML projects and write about them. This site is one of them — an automated daily AI digest.

repos.name: ai-weekly-report
repos.desc: My personalized AI newsletter that runs on an agentic AI

repos.name: Ace-rentals
repos.desc: Ace Rentals: A full-stack property rental platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features user authentication, image uploads, geocoding, reviews, and secure account management. Inspired by Airbnb.
